title: Partition Equal Subset Sum (DP- 15)
content:
Problem Description: Given an array arr of n integers, return true if the array can be partitioned into two subsets such that the sum of elements in both subsets is equal else return false.

Examples 
Input: nums = [2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5]
Output: True
Explanation: Nums can be partitioned into two subsets of sum 10.



Input: nums = [1, 2, 3, 5]
Output: False 
Explanation: The array cannot be partitioned into equal sum subsets.

Disclaimer: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

Memoization
Algorithm
This question is a slight modification of the problem discussed in Subset-sum equal to target. We need to partition the array into two subsets such that the sum of two subests is equal.

In other words, we can say that we need to find a subset in array which has sum equal to half of the sum of all the elements of the array. This is because, if any subset has half the sum of the array, then the remaining elements will also have the rest half of the sum of array, making the sum of both the subsets equal.
  • Calculate the total sum; if it is odd, return false.
  • Set the target as half of the total sum.
  • Use recursion to check if a subset can reach the target by including or skipping each element.
  • At each step, branch into two choices: include the current element (subtract its value from the remaining sum) or skip the current element (keep the remaining sum unchanged).
  • Memoize results using the current index and remaining sum to avoid recomputation.
  • If remaining sum becomes zero, return true; if it becomes negative or index exceeds bounds, return false.
  • Start from index 0 with target sum and return the stored or computed result.
Code
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253545556575859606162636465
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Function to check if it's possible to partition the array into two subsets with equal sum
bool subsetSumUtil(int ind, int target, vector<int>& arr, vector<vector<int>>& dp) {
    // Base case: If the target sum is 0, we found a valid partition
    if (target == 0)
        return true;

    // Base case: If we have considered all elements and the target is still not 0, return false
    if (ind == 0)
        return arr[0] == target;

    // If the result for this state is already calculated, return it
    if (dp[ind][target] != -1)
        return dp[ind][target];

    // Recursive cases
    // 1. Exclude the current element
    bool notTaken = subsetSumUtil(ind - 1, target, arr, dp);

    // 2. Include the current element if it doesn't exceed the target
    bool taken = false;
    if (arr[ind] <= target)
        taken = subsetSumUtil(ind - 1, target - arr[ind], arr, dp);

    // Store the result in the DP table and return
    return dp[ind][target] = notTaken || taken;
}

// Function to check if the array can be partitioned into two equal subsets
bool canPartition(int n, vector<int>& arr) {
    int totSum = 0;

    // Calculate the total sum of the array
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        totSum += arr[i];
    }

    // If the total sum is odd, it cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets
    if (totSum % 2 == 1)
        return false;
    else {
        int k = totSum / 2;

        // Create a DP table with dimensions n x k+1 and initialize with -1
        vector<vector<int>> dp(n, vector<int>(k + 1, -1));

        // Call the subsetSumUtil function to check if it's possible to partition
        return subsetSumUtil(n - 1, k, arr, dp);
    }
}

int main() {
    vector<int> arr = {2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5};
    int n = arr.size();

    if (canPartition(n, arr))
        cout << "The Array can be partitioned into two equal subsets";
    else
        cout << "The Array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets";

    return 0;
}
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253545556575859606162636465666768697071727374
import java.util.*;

class Solution {
    // Helper method to check if subset with target sum exists
    private boolean subsetSumUtil(int ind, int target, int[] arr, int[][] dp) {
        // Base case: target sum reached
        if (target == 0)
            return true;

        // Base case: first element check
        if (ind == 0)
            return arr[0] == target;

        // If already computed, return stored result
        if (dp[ind][target] != -1)
            return dp[ind][target] == 1;

        // Choice 1: exclude current element
        boolean notTaken = subsetSumUtil(ind - 1, target, arr, dp);

        // Choice 2: include current element if possible
        boolean taken = false;
        if (arr[ind] <= target)
            taken = subsetSumUtil(ind - 1, target - arr[ind], arr, dp);

        // Store result in dp table
        dp[ind][target] = (notTaken || taken) ? 1 : 0;
        return notTaken || taken;
    }

    // Main function to check if array can be partitioned into two equal subsets
    public boolean canPartition(int[] arr) {
        // Find array size
        int n = arr.length;

        // Calculate total sum
        int totSum = 0;
        for (int num : arr)
            totSum += num;

        // If sum is odd, partition not possible
        if (totSum % 2 == 1)
            return false;

        // Target sum for each subset
        int k = totSum / 2;

        // DP table initialization
        int[][] dp = new int[n][k + 1];
        for (int[] row : dp)
            Arrays.fill(row, -1);

        // Check possibility using recursion + memoization
        return subsetSumUtil(n - 1, k, arr, dp);
    }
}

// Separate Main class to run the program
public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        // Example array
        int[] arr = {2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5};

        // Create object of Solution class
        Solution obj = new Solution();

        // Output result
        if (obj.canPartition(arr))
            System.out.println("The Array can be partitioned into two equal subsets");
        else
            System.out.println("The Array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets");
    }
}

class Solution:
    # Helper function to check if subset with target sum exists
    def subsetSumUtil(self, ind, target, arr, dp):
        # Base case: target sum reached
        if target == 0:
            return True

        # Base case: first element check
        if ind == 0:
            return arr[0] == target

        # If already computed, return stored result
        if dp[ind][target] != -1:
            return dp[ind][target] == 1

        # Choice 1: exclude current element
        notTaken = self.subsetSumUtil(ind - 1, target, arr, dp)

        # Choice 2: include current element if possible
        taken = False
        if arr[ind] <= target:
            taken = self.subsetSumUtil(ind - 1, target - arr[ind], arr, dp)

        # Store result in dp table
        dp[ind][target] = 1 if (notTaken or taken) else 0
        return notTaken or taken

    # Main function to check if array can be partitioned into two equal subsets
    def canPartition(self, arr):
        # Find array size
        n = len(arr)

        # Calculate total sum
        totSum = sum(arr)

        # If sum is odd, partition not possible
        if totSum % 2 == 1:
            return False

        # Target sum for each subset
        k = totSum // 2

        # DP table initialization
        dp = [[-1] * (k + 1) for _ in range(n)]

        # Check possibility using recursion + memoization
        return self.subsetSumUtil(n - 1, k, arr, dp)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example array
    arr = [2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5]

    # Create object
    obj = Solution()

    # Output result
    if obj.canPartition(arr):
        print("The Array can be partitioned into two equal subsets")
    else:
        print("The Array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets")
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657
class Solution {
    // Helper method to check if subset with target sum exists
    subsetSumUtil(ind, target, arr, dp) {
        // Base case: target sum reached
        if (target === 0) return true;

        // Base case: first element check
        if (ind === 0) return arr[0] === target;

        // If already computed, return stored result
        if (dp[ind][target] !== -1) return dp[ind][target] === 1;

        // Choice 1: exclude current element
        let notTaken = this.subsetSumUtil(ind - 1, target, arr, dp);

        // Choice 2: include current element if possible
        let taken = false;
        if (arr[ind] <= target) {
            taken = this.subsetSumUtil(ind - 1, target - arr[ind], arr, dp);
        }

        // Store result in dp table
        dp[ind][target] = (notTaken || taken) ? 1 : 0;
        return notTaken || taken;
    }

    // Main function to check if array can be partitioned into two equal subsets
    canPartition(arr) {
        // Find array size
        const n = arr.length;

        // Calculate total sum
        let totSum = arr.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);

        // If sum is odd, partition not possible
        if (totSum % 2 === 1) return false;

        // Target sum for each subset
        const k = Math.floor(totSum / 2);

        // DP table initialization
        let dp = Array.from({ length: n }, () => Array(k + 1).fill(-1));

        // Check possibility using recursion + memoization
        return this.subsetSumUtil(n - 1, k, arr, dp);
    }
}

// Example usage
const arr = [2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5];
const obj = new Solution();

if (obj.canPartition(arr))
    console.log("The Array can be partitioned into two equal subsets");
else
    console.log("The Array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets");
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N*K), there are total N*K states, where N is the length of array and K is the target sum i.e. Half of the sum of all elements of the array.
Space Complexity: O(N*K) + O(N), we use a memo table to avoid recomputation. Extra auxiliary stack space is used for recursion.

Tabulation
Algorithm
In order to avoid extra recursive stack space used in memoization approach, we can simply convert our solution into tabulation approach. We can follow these steps in order to form our tabulation solution:
  • Create a 2D table of boolean values with dimensions matching the memoization approach, initially setting all entries to false.
  • Initialize the first column to true, since achieving a target sum of zero is always possible regardless of the number of elements considered.
  • For the first row, mark as true only the position corresponding to the first element’s value, if it does not exceed the target.
  • Use nested loops to fill the table row by row, applying the same inclusion–exclusion logic from the recursive solution but replacing recursive calls with table lookups.
  • Continue filling the table until all states are computed.
  • The answer will be found at the position in the table corresponding to using all elements to achieve the required target sum.
Code
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263646566
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    // Determines if the given array can be split into two subsets
    // whose sums are equal using a tabulation DP approach
    bool canPartition(int n, vector<int>& arr) {
        
        // Step 1: Calculate the total sum
        int totalSum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            totalSum += arr[i];
        }

        // Step 2: If total sum is odd, partition is impossible
        if (totalSum % 2 != 0) {
            return false;
        }

        // Step 3: Determine the target sum for each subset
        int targetSum = totalSum / 2;

        // Step 4: Create DP table and initialize
        vector<vector<bool>> dp(n, vector<bool>(targetSum + 1, false));

        // Step 5: Base case: sum 0 is always possible
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            dp[i][0] = true;
        }

        // Step 6: Initialize first row
        if (arr[0] <= targetSum) {
            dp[0][arr[0]] = true;
        }

        // Step 7: Fill DP table
        for (int index = 1; index < n; index++) {
            for (int target = 1; target <= targetSum; target++) {
                bool notTaken = dp[index - 1][target];
                bool taken = false;
                if (arr[index] <= target) {
                    taken = dp[index - 1][target - arr[index]];
                }
                dp[index][target] = notTaken || taken;
            }
        }

        // Step 8: Return result
        return dp[n - 1][targetSum];
    }
};

int main() {
    vector<int> arr = {2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5};
    int n = arr.size();
    Solution solver;

    if (solver.canPartition(n, arr)) {
        cout << "The array can be partitioned into two equal subsets";
    } else {
        cout << "The array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets";
    }
    return 0;
}

class Solution {
    // Determines if the given array can be split into two subsets
    // whose sums are equal using a tabulation DP approach
    public boolean canPartition(int[] arr) {
        int n = arr.length;

        // Step 1: Calculate total sum
        int totalSum = 0;
        for (int num : arr) totalSum += num;

        // Step 2: If total sum is odd, partition is impossible
        if (totalSum % 2 != 0) return false;

        // Step 3: Target sum for each subset
        int targetSum = totalSum / 2;

        // Step 4: Create DP table and initialize
        boolean[][] dp = new boolean[n][targetSum + 1];

        // Step 5: Base case: sum 0 is always possible
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) dp[i][0] = true;

        // Step 6: Initialize first row
        if (arr[0] <= targetSum) dp[0][arr[0]] = true;

        // Step 7: Fill DP table
        for (int index = 1; index < n; index++) {
            for (int target = 1; target <= targetSum; target++) {
                boolean notTaken = dp[index - 1][target];
                boolean taken = false;
                if (arr[index] <= target) {
                    taken = dp[index - 1][target - arr[index]];
                }
                dp[index][target] = notTaken || taken;
            }
        }

        // Step 8: Return result
        return dp[n - 1][targetSum];
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        int[] arr = {2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5};
        Solution solver = new Solution();

        if (solver.canPartition(arr))
            System.out.println("The array can be partitioned into two equal subsets");
        else
            System.out.println("The array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets");
    }
}

class Solution:
    # Determines if the given array can be split into two subsets
    # whose sums are equal using a tabulation DP approach
    def canPartition(self, arr):
        n = len(arr)

        # Step 1: Calculate total sum
        totalSum = sum(arr)

        # Step 2: If total sum is odd, partition is impossible
        if totalSum % 2 != 0:
            return False

        # Step 3: Target sum for each subset
        targetSum = totalSum // 2

        # Step 4: Create DP table and initialize
        dp = [[False] * (targetSum + 1) for _ in range(n)]

        # Step 5: Base case: sum 0 is always possible
        for i in range(n):
            dp[i][0] = True

        # Step 6: Initialize first row
        if arr[0] <= targetSum:
            dp[0][arr[0]] = True

        # Step 7: Fill DP table
        for index in range(1, n):
            for target in range(1, targetSum + 1):
                notTaken = dp[index - 1][target]
                taken = False
                if arr[index] <= target:
                    taken = dp[index - 1][target - arr[index]]
                dp[index][target] = notTaken or taken

        # Step 8: Return result
        return dp[n - 1][targetSum]

// Determines if the given array can be split into two subsets
// whose sums are equal using a tabulation DP approach
function canPartition(arr) {
    const n = arr.length;

    // Step 1: Calculate total sum
    let totalSum = arr.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);

    // Step 2: If total sum is odd, partition is impossible
    if (totalSum % 2 !== 0) return false;

    // Step 3: Target sum for each subset
    const targetSum = Math.floor(totalSum / 2);

    // Step 4: Create DP table and initialize
    const dp = Array.from({ length: n }, () => Array(targetSum + 1).fill(false));

    // Step 5: Base case: sum 0 is always possible
    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) dp[i][0] = true;

    // Step 6: Initialize first row
    if (arr[0] <= targetSum) dp[0][arr[0]] = true;

    // Step 7: Fill DP table
    for (let index = 1; index < n; index++) {
        for (let target = 1; target <= targetSum; target++) {
            let notTaken = dp[index - 1][target];
            let taken = false;
            if (arr[index] <= target) {
                taken = dp[index - 1][target - arr[index]];
            }
            dp[index][target] = notTaken || taken;
        }
    }

    // Step 8: Return result
    return dp[n - 1][targetSum];
}

// Example usage
const arr = [2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5];
if (canPartition(arr))
    console.log("The array can be partitioned into two equal subsets");
else
    console.log("The array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets");
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N*K), there are total N*K states, where N is the length of array and K is the target sum i.e. Half of the sum of all elements of the array.
Space Complexity: O(N*K) , we use a 2D DP array to avoid recomputation.

Space Optimization
Algorithm
If we closely look the relation, dp[ind][target] = dp[ind-1][target] || dp[ind-1][target-arr[ind]]
We see that to calculate a value of a cell of the dp array, we need only the previous row values

This means we do not need to store the entire 2D table, and instead can maintain just two 1D arrays, one representing the previous row and one for the current row. We initialize the first element of both arrays to true, as a sum of zero is always possible. For each new element in the array, we create and fill the current row based on the previous row’s values, then replace the previous row with the current one. After processing all elements, the answer will be found in the last updated previous row at the index equal to the target sum.

Code
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849505152535455565758596061626364656667
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Function to check if it's possible to partition the array into two subsets with equal sum
bool canPartition(int n, vector<int>& arr) {
    int totSum = 0;

    // Calculate the total sum of the array
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        totSum += arr[i];
    }

    // If the total sum is odd, it cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets
    if (totSum % 2 == 1)
        return false;
    else {
        int k = totSum / 2;

        // Create a vector to represent the previous row of the DP table
        vector<bool> prev(k + 1, false);

        // Base case: If the target sum is 0, it can be achieved by not selecting any elements
        prev[0] = true;

        // Initialize the first row based on the first element of the array
        if (arr[0] <= k)
            prev[arr[0]] = true;

        // Fill in the DP table using a bottom-up approach
        for (int ind = 1; ind < n; ind++) {
            // Create a vector to represent the current row of the DP table
            vector<bool> cur(k + 1, false);
            cur[0] = true;

            for (int target = 1; target <= k; target++) {
                // Exclude the current element
                bool notTaken = prev[target];

                // Include the current element if it doesn't exceed the target
                bool taken = false;
                if (arr[ind] <= target)
                    taken = prev[target - arr[ind]];

                // Update the current row of the DP table
                cur[target] = notTaken || taken;
            }

            // Set the current row as the previous row for the next iteration
            prev = cur;
        }

        // The final result is in the last cell of the previous row of the DP table
        return prev[k];
    }
}

int main() {
    vector<int> arr = {2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5};
    int n = arr.size();

    if (canPartition(n, arr))
        cout << "The Array can be partitioned into two equal subsets";
    else
        cout << "The Array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets";

    return 0;
}
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263
class Solution {
    // Checks if the given array can be partitioned into two subsets
    // such that the sum of elements in both subsets is equal
    public boolean canPartition(int[] arr) {
        int n = arr.length;

        // Calculate total sum of the array
        int totalSum = 0;
        for (int num : arr) totalSum += num;

        // If the sum is odd, partition into equal subsets is impossible
        if (totalSum % 2 != 0)
            return false;

        int target = totalSum / 2;

        // prev[sum] indicates if a subset with 'sum' is possible so far
        boolean[] prev = new boolean[target + 1];
        prev[0] = true; // sum = 0 is always possible

        // Initialize with the first element
        if (arr[0] <= target)
            prev[arr[0]] = true;

        // Iterate over all remaining elements
        for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
            // cur[sum] will store possibilities for current element
            boolean[] cur = new boolean[target + 1];
            cur[0] = true; // sum 0 always possible

            for (int sum = 1; sum <= target; sum++) {
                // Option 1: Do not take current element
                boolean notTaken = prev[sum];

                // Option 2: Take current element if it fits
                boolean taken = false;
                if (arr[i] <= sum)
                    taken = prev[sum - arr[i]];

                // Current sum possible if either option is true
                cur[sum] = notTaken || taken;
            }

            // Move to the next element
            prev = cur;
        }

        // Final answer is whether target sum is possible
        return prev[target];
    }
}

// Main class
public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        int[] arr = {2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5};
        Solution obj = new Solution();
        if (obj.canPartition(arr))
            System.out.println("The Array can be partitioned into two equal subsets");
        else
            System.out.println("The Array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets");
    }
}

class Solution:
    # Checks if the given array can be partitioned into two subsets
    # such that the sum of elements in both subsets is equal
    def canPartition(self, arr):
        n = len(arr)

        # Calculate total sum of the array
        totalSum = sum(arr)

        # If the sum is odd, partition into equal subsets is impossible
        if totalSum % 2 != 0:
            return False

        target = totalSum // 2

        # prev[sum] indicates if a subset with 'sum' is possible so far
        prev = [False] * (target + 1)
        prev[0] = True  # sum = 0 is always possible

        # Initialize with the first element
        if arr[0] <= target:
            prev[arr[0]] = True

        # Iterate over all remaining elements
        for i in range(1, n):
            # cur[sum] will store possibilities for current element
            cur = [False] * (target + 1)
            cur[0] = True  # sum 0 always possible

            # Check all possible target sums
            for s in range(1, target + 1):
                # Option 1: Do not take current element
                notTaken = prev[s]

                # Option 2: Take current element if it fits
                taken = False
                if arr[i] <= s:
                    taken = prev[s - arr[i]]

                # Current sum possible if either option is true
                cur[s] = notTaken or taken

            # Move to the next element
            prev = cur

        # Final answer is whether target sum is possible
        return prev[target]

    print("The Array can be partitioned into two equal subsets")
else:
    print("The Array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets")

// Checks if the given array can be partitioned into two subsets
// such that the sum of elements in both subsets is equal
function canPartition(arr) {
    const n = arr.length;

    // Calculate total sum of the array
    let totalSum = arr.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);

    // If the sum is odd, partition into equal subsets is impossible
    if (totalSum % 2 !== 0) return false;

    const target = totalSum / 2;

    // prev[sum] indicates if a subset with 'sum' is possible so far
    let prev = Array(target + 1).fill(false);
    prev[0] = true; // sum = 0 is always possible

    // Initialize with the first element
    if (arr[0] <= target) prev[arr[0]] = true;

    // Iterate over all remaining elements
    for (let i = 1; i < n; i++) {
        let cur = Array(target + 1).fill(false);
        cur[0] = true; // sum 0 always possible

        for (let sum = 1; sum <= target; sum++) {
            // Option 1: Do not take current element
            let notTaken = prev[sum];

            // Option 2: Take current element if it fits
            let taken = false;
            if (arr[i] <= sum) taken = prev[sum - arr[i]];

            // Current sum possible if either option is true
            cur[sum] = notTaken || taken;
        }

        // Move to the next element
        prev = cur;
    }

    // Final answer is whether target sum is possible
    return prev[target];
}

// Example usage
let arr = [2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5];
if (canPartition(arr))
    console.log("The Array can be partitioned into two equal subsets");
else
    console.log("The Array cannot be partitioned into two equal subsets");
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N*K), there are total N*K states, where N is the length of array and K is the target sum i.e. Half of the sum of all elements of the array.
Space Complexity: O(N), we use two 1D arrays to store value of previous row and current row.
